Title: GhMYB102 affects cotton fibre elongation and secondary wall thickening by regulating GhIRX10 in cotton

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes xylan synthesis, increases fibre secondary cell wall thickness and strength, and reduces fibre length when overexpressed; opposite trend for xylan content in RNAi lines.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major hemicellulose component of the secondary cell wall that contributes to fibre wall formation and mechanical strength.
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: xylan biosynthesis / secondary cell wall polysaccharide biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyses de novo elongation of the xylan backbone by adding beta-1,4-linked xylosyl residues during xylan synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control step for xylan backbone synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GT47 glycosyltransferase family; N-terminal transmembrane domain
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in 20–25 DPA fibres; GhIRX10_A03 expressed more strongly in fibres than GhIRX10_D02; negatively correlated with fibre length.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in 20–25 DPA fibres during secondary wall thickening.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: field-grown plants in Anyang; developmental stage 20–25 DPA fibres is critical for interpretation
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S-based overexpression vector pBI121; RNAi construct in pHELLSGATE4
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: transgenic line raw data PRJNA1144248; fibre developmental transcriptome PRJNA490626 and PRJNA248163
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression; RNAi silencing; HPLC quantification of xylo-oligosaccharides; fibre quality phenotyping; SEM/cross-sections; subcellular localization; RNA-seq; RT-qPCR
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhIRX10_A03 (GH_A03G1759) is the major fibre-expressed IRX10 homologue in cotton. 2. GhIRX10 localizes to the Golgi and contains an N-terminal transmembrane domain required for proper localization. 3. Overexpression increases xylo-oligosaccharide content, cell wall thickness and fibre strength, but shortens fibres. 4. RNAi reduces xylan-associated output and alters fibre morphology. 5. GhIRX10 expression is directly activated by GhMYB102.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to modulate fibre wall thickening and strength, but overexpression causes shorter fibres; should be tuned or combined with elongation-supporting alleles rather than constitutively overexpressed alone.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced fibre length with overexpression; altered mature fibre morphology and possible imbalance between elongation and wall thickening.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Four cotton IRX10 homologues were identified; GhIRX10_A03 was chosen for detailed study because of stronger fibre expression. The paper discusses likely functional association with the IRX9/IRX14 xylan synthase complex, although direct physical interaction was not tested here.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activates secondary cell wall polysaccharide synthesis, increasing cellulose and hemicellulose accumulation, increasing fibre wall thickness and strength, and reducing fibre length.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Structural polysaccharides of the cotton fibre secondary cell wall that determine wall thickening and fibre mechanical quality.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: R2R3-MYB transcription factor with two N-terminal SNAT DNA-binding conserved domains
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: secondary cell wall biosynthesis; cellulose biosynthesis; hemicellulose/xylan biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Critical positive regulator of fibre secondary wall thickening that directly activates GhIRX10 and GhCESA4/7/8 and broadly promotes SCW-related genes.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates GhIRX10 via TTAGGT motif in its promoter; directly binds and activates GhCESA4/7/8 promoters; binds GhFSN1 promoter and is associated with upregulation of many SCW-related genes including IRX9, IRX7, GUX1, GUX2, TBLs, XXT2, XTH30 and KATAMARI1.
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Reciprocal regulatory loop with GhFSN1; evidence also suggests feedback induction of GhMYB102 when GhIRX10 is silenced.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Direct activation by GhFSN1 and GhMYB7
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expression profile closely matches GhIRX10 and is associated with fibre secondary wall thickening stages.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in fibres; overexpression causes SCW-thickening phenotype; upregulated in RNAi-GhIRX10 lines.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: field-grown plants; developmental regulation during fibre SCW thickening
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: DAP-seq; RNA-seq PRJNA1144248; fibre developmental transcriptome PRJNA490626 and PRJNA248163
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression; Y1H; dual-luciferase assays; EMSA; DAP-seq; RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; cellulose and hemicellulose assays; fibre quality phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhMYB102 was identified as the MYB factor matching the TTAGGT motif in the GhIRX10 promoter and with a similar fibre expression pattern. 2. GhMYB102 directly binds the GhIRX10 promoter and activates its transcription. 3. Overexpression of GhMYB102 increases cellulose and hemicellulose contents, thickens fibre cell walls, increases strength, and shortens fibres. 4. DAP-seq and RNA-seq show that GhMYB102 controls a broad SCW gene network. 5. GhMYB102 directly activates GhCESA4/7/8 and reciprocally regulates GhFSN1, while also being positively regulated by GhFSN1 and GhMYB7.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising regulatory target for increasing fibre strength and wall deposition, but constitutive upregulation penalizes fibre length and some fibre quality parameters; stage-specific or moderate expression engineering may be preferable.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced fibre length; increased micronaire; reduced uniformity or elongation in some OE lines; possible competitive interaction with GhMYB46 in the SCW network.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: GhMYB102 binding motif contains core TTAGGT sequence. The paper proposes that GhMYB102 and GhMYB46 may act both synergistically and competitively in SCW regulation.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive upstream regulator of GhMYB102, thereby promoting secondary cell wall biosynthetic gene expression and fibre wall thickening network output.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Structural support of cotton fibre secondary walls.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NAC domain transcription factor; cotton homologue of AtNST1
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: secondary cell wall biosynthesis regulatory network
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Acts as an upstream positive regulator of GhMYB102 and participates in a reciprocal regulatory loop connected to SCW synthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates GhMYB102 expression by binding NBS elements in the GhMYB102 promoter.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in OE-GhMYB102 lines according to RNA-seq and RT-qPCR; prior OE-GhFSN1 data showed GhMYB102 upregulation.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq citation from prior study; RT-qPCR in this study
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: DLR; EMSA; expression analysis in OE-GhMYB102 and cited OE-GhFSN1 lines
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhFSN1 directly binds the GhMYB102 promoter at NAC-binding sites. 2. GhFSN1 activates GhMYB102 transcription. 3. GhFSN1 itself is upregulated in OE-GhMYB102, indicating a reciprocal positive regulatory relationship.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential upstream engineering node for SCW enhancement, but likely to share wall-thickening versus fibre-length tradeoffs of the downstream network.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core regulator because this study experimentally validated its direct regulatory link with GhMYB102 in the results section.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive upstream regulator of GhMYB102 within the fibre secondary wall regulatory network.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Structural support of cotton fibre secondary walls.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: secondary cell wall biosynthesis regulatory network
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Upstream activator contributing to GhMYB102-dependent SCW synthesis regulation.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates the GhMYB102 promoter through SMRE-like elements.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Prior RNAi-GhMYB7 data showed GhMYB102 downregulation.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: cited prior RNA-seq/expression evidence; DLR in this study
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: DLR plus cited RNAi-GhMYB7 expression evidence
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhMYB7 positively regulates GhMYB102 transcription. 2. GhMYB102 expression decreases in RNAi-GhMYB7 material from prior work. 3. This places GhMYB7 upstream of the GhMYB102-GhIRX10 module in the SCW network.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible upstream regulator for coordinated SCW engineering, though direct metabolic outcomes were not newly tested here.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because the study experimentally confirmed promoter activation of GhMYB102 by GhMYB7, although much evidence is integrated with prior work.
